goal: Make Foreman production-ready E2E with layered agents (CEO/CTO/eng) and DoDs at every level.
summary: ship Foreman as a pilot-grade product: CEO -> CTO subplanner -> eng workers, DoDs at every layer
acceptanceCriteria: CEO DoD #1: Public URLs healthy on a cold cellular phone -- https://foreman-phi.vercel.app loads, /api/health returns 200 ok, Render /health returns 200 ok, /api/voice/config reports liveAvailable=true.

CEO DoD #2: End-to-end customer walk works from a fresh phone: consent -> Start job -> camera frame analysed -> microphone phrase transcribed -> Stop job -> non-fallback session summary returned -> row counts > 0 for sessions/frames/coaching_events/transcript_segments in Supabase.

CEO DoD #3: Security posture is clean: no committed secrets in current HEAD or last 50 commits (gitleaks reports zero leaks), production boot refuses to start without FOREMAN_API_KEY/CORS_ORIGINS, no NEXT_PUBLIC_* AI keys, CORS allowlist strict, rate limits effective on AI routes, Vercel /api/* origin gate active.

CEO DoD #4: Privacy + consent UX clear -- the web client gates capture behind an explicit consent step, a visible recording indicator appears while a session is active, and the consent copy points at the AU privacy / sensitive-data treatment described in CLAUDE.md.

CEO DoD #5: Cost guards documented and enforced -- per-route rate limits on /analyse, /transcribe, /voice/*, frame size cap enforced server-side (>= existing client compression target), and an operator-facing note in DEPLOY.md/SECURITY.md spells out billing alert setup for Anthropic + OpenAI.

CEO DoD #6: Observability minimum -- /ready endpoint exposes anthropic/openai/supabase availability booleans, every 5xx response includes a server-side correlation id in logs, the Fastify logger emits structured JSON in production, and DEPLOY.md documents where to read Render + Vercel logs.

CEO DoD #7: Tests + readiness scripts pass -- `npm run build` in monorepo is clean, `npm test` passes in backend and web workspaces, `bash scripts/check-ready.sh` reports green when env is set, and there is at least one E2E smoke script that walks /sessions/start -> /analyse -> /transcribe -> /sessions/:id/stop against a configurable BASE_URL.

CEO DoD #8: Docs match reality -- README, DEPLOY.md, PHONE_DEMO.md, SECURITY.md, YOUR_ACTIONS.md state only claims backed by code (or explicitly mark them TODO), Render/Vercel URLs are correct, and the Render free-tier cold-start caveat is called out where it matters.

CEO DoD #9: iOS / glasses path scaffold is not regressed -- `native/ios` opens via xcodegen and references the DAT 0.7 SDK as the conventions document; remaining gaps are listed in a follow-up file rather than failing the build.

tasks.name: cto-readiness
tasks.scopedGoal: You are the CTO. Own the technical realisation of every CEO DoD item in `acceptanceCriteria` of the parent plan.json. Decompose into eng workers + verifiers under your subtree (further sub-subplanners are allowed if a slice is large). Your own CTO DoD must be written into your plan.json `acceptanceCriteria` and must cover at minimum: (a) backend auth/CORS/rate-limit/error-handling hardening with tests, (b) /ready endpoint exposes anthropic+openai+supabase booleans and structured JSON logging in production, (c) AI-route rate limits + payload size enforcement + correlation IDs, (d) web client consent gating, visible recording indicator, error boundaries, and `same-origin` API proxy verified, (e) Supabase RLS + storage policy audit confirmed against `backend/supabase/`, (f) repo-wide secret/leak audit (current HEAD + recent history) using gitleaks or equivalent, (g) end-to-end smoke script in `scripts/` that walks /sessions/start -> /analyse -> /transcribe -> /sessions/:id/stop against `${BASE_URL}` with a FOREMAN_API_KEY, (h) docs truth-sync across README/DEPLOY/SECURITY/PHONE_DEMO/YOUR_ACTIONS removing or marking any unbacked claims, (i) native/ios compile sanity (xcodegen + xcodebuild dry-run on simulator destination if toolchain is available, otherwise document the gap in `native/ios/README.md`). Each child task you publish MUST carry its own engineering DoD in `acceptance` (concrete, verifiable). Default to broad workers over many tiny ones; pair every behaviour-changing worker with a verifier. Keep merge tasks explicit (`type: worker` with `dependsOn` of sibling branches). MODEL CONSTRAINTS (verified by `bun cli.ts models --check` against the active CURSOR_API_KEY): only these slugs are valid on this key -- `claude-opus-4-7` (use for sub-subplanners and verifiers), `gpt-5.3-codex-high-fast` (use for code-implementation workers), and `composer-2-fast` (use for bounded straight-line workers). DO NOT pick `claude-opus-4-7-thinking-xhigh`, `gpt-5.5-high-fast`, `gpt-5.5-high`, `opus-max`, or `gpt-xhigh` -- the SDK rejects them with `invalid_model`. Always set `tasks[].model` explicitly on every child you publish (do not rely on the default-for-type, since the subplanner default is rejected on this key). Do not open PRs from inside the subtree; the root opens the PR. Your final handoff aggregates your subtree onto a single deliverable branch.
acceptance: CTO DoD: every CEO DoD item in the parent plan's `acceptanceCriteria` is either delivered on your handoff branch or has a documented, planner-visible reason it is blocked by user-only action (e.g. needs new third-party API key); blocked items are listed verbatim in `## Notes, concerns` with the exact action the human must take.
CTO DoD: monorepo `npm run build` is clean on your handoff branch.
CTO DoD: `cd backend && npm test` and `cd web && npm test` both pass on your handoff branch.

CTO DoD: a runnable smoke script exists at `scripts/smoke-e2e.sh` (or `scripts/smoke-e2e.ts`) that walks /sessions/start -> /analyse with a fixture image -> /transcribe with a fixture audio clip -> /sessions/:id/stop against `${BASE_URL:-http://127.0.0.1:8080}` using `${FOREMAN_API_KEY}`, exiting non-zero on any failure.
CTO DoD: gitleaks (or equivalent) shows zero high-severity leaks against current HEAD; the CI workflow already added previously is wired to run on push and PR.
CTO DoD: `/ready` returns `{ ok, anthropic, openai, supabase, elevenlabs?, transcription }` (or equivalent) so the readiness script and Render health probes have a structured truthful signal.
CTO DoD: README, DEPLOY.md, PHONE_DEMO.md, SECURITY.md, YOUR_ACTIONS.md have been read end-to-end and each claim either backed by code on your branch or marked clearly as TODO.
CTO DoD: handoff branch is mergeable into `main` with no conflicts; consolidate via explicit merge worker tasks inside your subtree, not by asking the root to merge.
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `git checkout <cto-readiness branch>` and run `npm install`.
- Ensure local `backend/.env` has placeholder values so configured-check booleans flip predictably (or use the synthetic `/ready` path).

## Automated
- `npm run build` from repo root must succeed.
- `(cd backend && npm test)` and `(cd web && npm test)` must pass.
- `bash scripts/check-ready.sh` runs (it is acceptable for it to report `yellow` on missing keys, but it must not error on structural problems).
- Run any new `scripts/smoke-e2e.*` against a locally running backend (start the backend in another terminal first) and confirm it exits 0 when all keys are present, or that its missing-key error messaging is clear and non-zero exit.
- `gitleaks detect --no-banner --redact -s . --log-level info` (install with `brew install gitleaks` or `go install`) reports zero high-severity findings.

## Manual
- Curl the live deployment: `https://foreman-phi.vercel.app/api/health` -> 200 ok; `https://foreman-api-y31r.onrender.com/health` -> 200 ok; `https://foreman-phi.vercel.app/api/voice/config` reports `liveAvailable=true` (free-tier Render cold start may take ~60s).
- Read README, DEPLOY.md, PHONE_DEMO.md, SECURITY.md, YOUR_ACTIONS.md and spot-check that nothing is aspirational or wrong.

## Gotchas
- Render free tier sleeps after ~15 min idle; the first curl may take 30-60s.
- Do not attempt to rotate or set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / OPENAI_API_KEY / Supabase service role on this branch -- those are owned by the human operator. List any DoD items that depend on those secrets in your `## Notes` instead of failing the worker.
- iOS smoke is best-effort: if the cloud-agent VM has no `xcodebuild`, document the gap in `native/ios/README.md` rather than spinning on it.

tasks.name: verify-ceo-product-readiness
tasks.scopedGoal: Verify that the CTO's handoff branch satisfies every CEO DoD item in the parent plan's `acceptanceCriteria`. Run the full verification recipe in `verify`. For each CEO DoD bullet, record explicit pass / not met / n/a with the evidence command and its outcome. Cross-check that any DoD the CTO claims is blocked-by-human-action is genuinely a human-only step (e.g. needs a third-party secret rotation), and not engineering work the CTO skipped. Output your verdict as `## Verification` per the verifier prompt structure: prefer `live-ui-verified` only if you actually exercised the deployed phone web UI end-to-end; `unit-test-verified` if you ran the smoke script + unit tests; downgrade to `type-check-only` if the build was the only signal. If a DoD item is impossible to verify from inside the cloud-agent VM (e.g. iPhone hardware capture), mark it `n/a` and explain.
acceptance: Verification section lists evidence for every CEO DoD bullet in the parent plan's `acceptanceCriteria` (9 items).
If the CTO marked items as blocked-by-human-action, the verifier independently agreed (with one-line reasoning) or escalated by adding a `(high)` finding.
`## Verification` line uses one of the prescribed values (`live-ui-verified`, `unit-test-verified`, `type-check-only`, `verifier-blocked`, `verifier-failed`).
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- Inherits `cto-readiness` branch (auto-wired by orchestrate).
- `npm install` at repo root.

## Automated
- `npm run build`.
- `(cd backend && npm test)` and `(cd web && npm test)`.
- `bash scripts/check-ready.sh` (note env gaps but do not treat missing local API keys as failure if the production URLs verify).
- Run `scripts/smoke-e2e.*` against the production stack with `BASE_URL=https://foreman-phi.vercel.app/api FOREMAN_API_KEY=<test-key-if-provided>` -- if no test key is available, point at a locally booted backend instead and document.
- `gitleaks detect --no-banner --redact -s .` -> zero high findings.

## Manual / curl
- `curl -s https://foreman-phi.vercel.app/api/health` -> `{"status":"ok"}`.
- `curl -s https://foreman-api-y31r.onrender.com/health` -> `{"status":"ok"}`.
- `curl -s https://foreman-phi.vercel.app/api/voice/config` includes `liveAvailable:true`.
- Skim README/DEPLOY/SECURITY/PHONE_DEMO/YOUR_ACTIONS -- flag any claim that does not match the code on the branch.

## CEO DoD checklist (verifier MUST report per bullet)
For each of the 9 acceptanceCriteria bullets on the parent plan, write `- [x] <criterion text>: <evidence>` or `- [ ] <criterion text>: <reason not met>`.

## Gotchas
- The dispatcher's human operator owns secret rotation; do not block on missing ANTHROPIC/OPENAI/Supabase keys -- mark such items as `(verifier-blocked, awaiting human secret rotation)` instead of `(verifier-failed)` and explain.
- iPhone hardware smoke is `n/a` from inside this VM; rely on production URL responses + the smoke script.
